entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Novel super-early genotypes of Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp. characterized by photoperiod insensitivity

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD INSENSITIVITY
entities.description: The ability of a plant to flower and mature regardless of the day length, enabling growth in non-traditional regions

entities.name: NON-TRADITIONAL REGIONS
entities.description: Geographic areas where pigeonpea is not typically grown due to environmental constraints

entities.name: GENETIC PARAMETERS
entities.description: Statistical measures used to estimate the magnitude of variability and heritable components of variation

entities.name: SELECTION AND BREEDING STRATEGIES
entities.description: Approaches used to develop improved crop varieties based on the estimation of genetic parameters

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The total productivity of the pigeonpea genotypes

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL TRAITS
entities.description: Biological and functional characteristics of the plant including leaf area and assimilation

entities.name: QUALITY TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics that determine the nutritional or commercial value of the crop, such as protein content

entities.name: LEAF AREA DURATION
entities.description: The duration of leaf area maintained between 60 days after sowing and maturity

entities.name: LEAF AREA
entities.description: The total surface area of the leaves of the plant

entities.name: LEAF AREA INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of total upper leaf surface of a plant to the surface area of the land on which the plant grows

entities.name: NET ASSIMILATION
entities.description: The rate of carbon fixation and organic matter production between 60 days after sowing and maturity

entities.name: PLANT HEIGHT
entities.description: The vertical measurement of the plant from the ground to the highest point

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC COEFFICIENTS OF VARIATION
entities.description: A measure of the total variation observed in a trait, influenced by both genetics and environment

entities.name: GENOTYPIC COEFFICIENTS OF VARIATION
entities.description: A measure of the variation in a trait that is due solely to genetic differences

entities.name: ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: External factors that influence the expression of phenotypic traits and the PCV estimates

entities.name: HERITABILITY
entities.description: The proportion of phenotypic variation that is attributable to genetic factors

entities.name: GENETIC ADVANCE
entities.description: The expected improvement in a trait resulting from selection, often expressed as a percent of the mean

entities.name: PROTEIN CONTENT
entities.description: The amount of protein present in the pigeonpea seeds, used as a quality trait

entities.name: ADDITIVE GENE
entities.description: Genes that contribute to trait expression in a cumulative manner, governing heritability

entities.name: SELECTION INDEX
entities.description: A tool used to select genotypes based on a combination of multiple traits to improve overall yield and quality
